definitions.definition: A network is a collection of interconnected devices that communicate with each other. It can be a local or wide-area network, connected by a variety of physical or wireless links.
A node is a device connected to a network, such as a computer, printer, or router. It can be a data source, data sink, or both.
A link is a connection between two nodes in a network, allowing data to be transmitted between them.
A protocol is a set of rules and standards that govern data communication between nodes in a network.
TCP/IP (Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol) is a suite of communication protocols used to interconnect devices on the internet.
An IP address is a unique numerical address assigned to each device on a network, allowing data to be routed between them.
A MAC (Media Access Control) address is a unique identifier assigned to a network interface controller, used to identify a device on a network.
A router is a device that forwards data packets between networks, using routing tables to determine the best path.
A switch is a device that connects multiple nodes together, using MAC addresses to forward data packets between them.
A hub is a device that connects multiple nodes together, forwarding incoming data packets to all connected nodes.
A LAN (Local Area Network) is a network that spans a small geographical area, such as a home, office building, or campus.
A WAN (Wide Area Network) is a network that spans a large geographical area, such as a city or country.
A MAN (Metropolitan Area Network) is a network that spans a metropolitan area, such as a city or town.
A CAN (Campus Area Network) is a network that spans a campus or group of buildings.
A VPN (Virtual Private Network) is a network that uses encryption and tunneling to create a secure connection between nodes over the internet.
LAN switching is a technique used to forward data packets between nodes on a LAN, using MAC addresses to determine the destination node.
Routing is the process of forwarding data packets between nodes on different networks, using routing tables to determine the best path.
The internet is a global network of interconnected computers and servers, using standardized protocols to facilitate communication.
A packet is a unit of data transmitted over a network, consisting of a header and payload.
The data link layer is a layer of the OSI model responsible for data transfer between nodes on the same network.
Network topology refers to the physical or logical arrangement of nodes and links in a network.
Network architecture refers to the overall design and organization of a network, including the choice of technologies and protocols.
